# Phase 4 — Contrast Matrix

**Date:** 2026-04-29
**Generated by:** `/tmp/bb-audit/contrast-matrix.mjs`
**Source:** `frontend/src/app/globals.css`

Computed WCAG 2.x relative-luminance contrast for every (foreground, background) pair the design system actually uses. AA thresholds: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3.0:1 for large text (≥18pt or ≥14pt bold), 3.0:1 for non-text indicators (focus rings, icons).

### Light theme

| Pair | Use | Foreground | Background | Ratio | AA normal (≥4.5) | AA large (≥3.0) | Focus (≥3.0) |
|---|---|---|---|---:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
| Body text on page | Default body | `0 0% 4%` | `0 0% 100%` | 19.78 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Body text on card | Card content | `0 0% 4%` | `0 0% 100%` | 19.78 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Body text on elevated surface | Raised cards | `0 0% 4%` | `220 14% 96%` | 18.02 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Body text in popover/dialog | Modals, dropdowns | `0 0% 4%` | `0 0% 100%` | 19.78 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

---

## Reading the matrix

### "AA normal" failures that intentionally pass "AA large"

Solid state surfaces (`--destructive`, `--success` in dark, etc.) under their
state-foreground colors are saturated mid-tones that mathematically can't
reach 4.5:1 against any usable foreground without losing brand identity.
These render as **AA-large-only** passes, which is acceptable for button
and badge typography (≥14pt bold or ≥18pt regular). Body text on solid
state surfaces is **not** a supported pattern — see the cheatsheet rule
below.

### Soft-state surfaces — use `text-foreground` for inline body

`text-{state}` (e.g., `text-destructive`) on `bg-{state}-soft` is borderline
or below AA normal. For inline body text inside a soft state banner, prefer:

```tsx
<div className="bg-destructive-soft border border-destructive/30 text-foreground">
  <strong className="text-destructive">Heads up:</strong> message body…
</div>
```

The state hue communicates intent through the surface and the optional
heading; the body remains `text-foreground` for AA normal contrast.

### Focus rings — pass everywhere ≥3:1

`--focus-ring` reaches 4.94:1 against the page in light theme and well
above 3:1 in dark theme — comfortably above the WCAG 2.2 `focus-not-obscured`
threshold for non-text indicators.
